MonsterSurvivors.title: Monster Survivors Online: Survival Guide and Controls
MonsterSurvivors.summary: Survive waves, collect upgrades, and use this guide to make smarter movement, upgrade, and escape decisions during each browser run.

playSection.title: Play Monster Survivors
playSection.description: Launch the embedded browser version instantly.
playSection.hint: Use fullscreen mode for the best experience.

overview.title: Why Monster Survivors needs a plan
overview.description: A practical guide for players who want more than a quick launch button.

body.0: Monster Survivors is a short-run survival action game where the first mistake is usually standing still for too long. The screen fills with enemies, experience drops, and upgrade choices, so a good run depends on reading space before it disappears. Instead of chasing every pickup immediately, strong players create safe loops, keep an exit path open, and collect rewards when the crowd has shifted away from the danger zone.
body.1: The useful habit is to think in cycles. Move to thin out the nearest group, collect enough experience to trigger an upgrade, then choose upgrades that solve the current problem: wider area damage when enemies surround you, faster movement when the arena feels tight, or stronger single-target damage when larger enemies take too long to clear. This page keeps the rules, controls, and decision points visible so a new player can improve without leaving the game.

items.0: Keep moving before the arena fills up.
items.1: Prioritize upgrades that improve area control.
items.2: Collect experience safely instead of chasing every drop.
items.3: Save risky paths for moments when cooldowns are ready.

howToPlay.title: How to play Monster Survivors
howToPlay.description: Use a simple survival loop instead of reacting late.

steps.0: Start moving as soon as the round begins. Watch the closest enemy group first, then steer toward open space instead of the biggest pile of rewards.
steps.1: Collect experience when it is safe. If a drop is inside a crowded lane, circle around and return after the enemies spread out.
steps.2: Choose upgrades that match the board. Area control helps when small enemies swarm, while movement and cooldown upgrades help you escape tight corners.
steps.3: Do not trap yourself at the edge. Use the outside of the arena only when you already have a path back toward the center.

controls.title: Controls
controls.description: Desktop controls are the most reliable for this action game.

move.label: Move
move.value: Use the keyboard movement keys or on-screen controls, depending on the browser build, to keep distance from enemy groups.

aim.label: Positioning
aim.value: Most survival pressure is solved by positioning. Keep moving through gaps instead of waiting for enemies to reach you.

upgrades.label: Upgrades
upgrades.value: When an upgrade panel appears, choose the option that fixes your current weakness rather than the flashiest number.

fullscreen.label: Fullscreen
fullscreen.value: Use fullscreen on desktop so you can see enemy lanes earlier and avoid accidental edge traps.

faq.title: Monster Survivors FAQ
faq.description: Common questions before starting a run.

0.question: Is Monster Survivors better on desktop or mobile?
0.answer: Desktop is recommended because movement-heavy survival games are easier to read with more screen space and keyboard-style control.

1.question: What should I upgrade first?
1.answer: Early upgrades should help you control space. Area damage, cooldown reduction, and movement safety usually matter more than chasing one high-damage option.

2.question: Why do my runs end suddenly?
2.answer: Most early losses come from walking into a corner, chasing unsafe experience drops, or saving an escape route too late. Plan the next open lane before you need it.

3.question: Does this page require a download?
3.answer: No. The game runs in the browser page and this guide does not offer installers, APK files, ROMs, or cracked downloads.

Solitaire.title: Classic Solitaire Online: Play and Learn the Basics
Solitaire.summary: Play free online Solitaire directly in your browser, then use this guide to understand the rules, controls, and practical moves that keep a card layout open.

playSection.title: Play Free Online Solitaire
playSection.description: Start a clean classic card session instantly in your browser.
playSection.hint: Fullscreen mode gives more space for card movement.

overview.title: What makes this Solitaire page useful
overview.description: A quick guide for players who want more than an embedded card table.

body.0: Classic Solitaire is a quiet card puzzle built around small choices: reveal hidden tableau cards, keep columns flexible, and move cards to the foundations only when doing so opens the board. This browser version is best treated as a clean Klondike-style session for short breaks. You do not need an account or a download, and the page keeps the core rules visible so new players can start without leaving the game.
body.1: The main goal is to build four foundation piles from ace to king, one suit at a time. On the tableau, cards are arranged in descending order with alternating colors. A good round is rarely about speed; it is about preserving useful moves, checking whether a card should stay in the tableau, and using the stock pile only when the visible board has stopped offering progress.
body.2: Use this page as a reference while you play: review the controls, compare your next move with the tips, then jump to related no-download games when you want a different pace.

details.title: Game details
details.description: Quick facts before you start a classic solitaire round.

source.label: Play source
source.value: Embedded browser game source; no installer, APK, ROM, or cracked download is offered on Luma.

items.0: Reveal face-down cards before moving cards to foundations too early.
items.1: Keep empty columns available for kings when possible.
items.2: Cycle the stock carefully so you do not bury useful moves.
items.3: Undo and review when a layout stops progressing.

howToPlay.title: How to play Solitaire
howToPlay.description: Follow the board in a stable order so you do not miss simple moves.

steps.0: Scan the tableau first. Move exposed cards into descending, alternating-color stacks and prioritize moves that reveal a face-down card.
steps.1: Start foundation piles with aces, then add cards by suit in order. Avoid moving a card to a foundation if it blocks a useful tableau move.
steps.2: Use empty columns for kings or king-led stacks. An empty column is valuable because it can unlock longer rearrangements later.
steps.3: Cycle the stock after the tableau stalls. Before drawing again, check whether the current waste card can open a hidden card or complete a foundation move.

controls.title: Controls
controls.description: Simple browser controls for desktop and touch devices.

move.label: Move cards
move.value: Click or tap a card, then place it on a valid tableau stack, foundation pile, or open column.

stock.label: Draw stock
stock.value: Use the stock pile when no visible tableau move helps. Review the waste card before cycling again.

foundation.label: Build foundations
foundation.value: Send aces and same-suit sequences upward only when the move keeps the tableau flexible.

fullscreen.label: Fullscreen
fullscreen.value: Open fullscreen if cards feel cramped on a smaller display.

faq.title: Solitaire FAQ
faq.description: Common questions before starting a round.

0.question: What version of Solitaire is this?
0.answer: This page is written for classic Klondike-style Solitaire, the familiar layout with tableau columns, foundations, a stock pile, and a waste pile.

1.question: Can I play Solitaire on mobile?
1.answer: Yes. The game runs in a browser iframe and supports touch, though a tablet or desktop screen gives more room for reading card stacks.

2.question: What should I do when there are no obvious moves?
2.answer: Check hidden-card opportunities first, then look for an empty-column setup for a king. If the tableau is still locked, draw from the stock and reassess.

3.question: Is this page safe for short casual play?
3.answer: The page links to a browser-play source and does not offer ROMs, cracked downloads, installers, or gambling mechanics.
